title: Elements other than image (Tag: IMG) should not have alt attribute
aria-controls should reference a valid element id
aria-describedby should reference a valid element id
Form fields should indicate validation errors to assistive technology
Only input fields with validation errors should be assigned aria-invalid="true"
aria-labelledby should reference a valid element id
Only elements that function as articles should be tagged as article regions
Functional image displayed using CSS background properties should be tagged for assistive technology
Breadcrumbs navigation should be tagged properly
Breadcrumb navigation region should have a label
Aria describedby/labelledby must point to a valid, existing element ID
Buttons should have a label
Buttons should be tagged for assistive technology
An alternative modality should be provided for CAPTCHA challenges
CAPTCHA used in authentication should not involve cognitive function tests
Carousels should not be tagged as live regions
Carousels should have a descriptive label
Carousels that require dragging movements should provide alternative methods of operation with a single pointer
Carousels should be tagged for assistive technology
Carousel navigation next arrow should be labelled for assistive technology
Carousel Next pagination control should have an accurate and descriptive label
Carousel navigation previous arrows should be labelled for assistive technology
Carousel Previous pagination control should have an accurate and descriptive label
Carousels should be labelled properly without redundancy
Carousel pagination tabs should have accurate and descriptive labels
Checked state of custom checkbox controls should be exposed to assistive technology
Checkbox controls should have a label
Custom checkbox controls should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as checkbox controls should receive role="checkbox"
Custom checkboxes, radio buttons and file upload fields should be accessible to assistive technology
Custom select options lists should be tagged for assistive technology
Custom select triggers should be tagged for assistive technology
Keyboard focus should move into and lock inside active dialogs
Modal dialogs should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as dialogs should be tagged as dialog
The id attribute should have a unique value
Emphasis should be tagged properly for assistive technology
Figure elements should receive text description or lose figure role
Focused elements should not be obscured by a sticky footer
Focused elements should not be obscured by a sticky header
Focused elements should not be obscured by a sticky header
Footer navigation should be labelled properly
Interacting with form controls should not cause a change in context unless a user is notified beforehand
Forms and form fields must have unique ID attributes
A container of input elements should be tagged as a form
Form submission controls should have type="submit"
Page has a meta viewport
Header navigation should be labelled properly
Headings should not be empty
Each page should have a main heading
Heading should be concise
Headings should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as headings should be tagged as heading
Heading levels should follow a logical hierarchy
Heading levels should reflect the structure of the content
A page should not have more than one main heading
Default page language should be defined
HTML lang attribute should have a valid value
Meaningful icons should have a label, while decorative icons should be hidden
Iframes should have a descriptive label
Functional images should have a text alternative
Functional images should have an informative and accurate text alternative
Images should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as images should be tagged as image
Inner content navigation should be labelled properly
Input controls should have a descriptive label
Interactive elements should be keyboard navigable
Targets for interactive elements should meet a minimum size (at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels) or have enough spacing around them
Letter spacing should scale to at least 0.12 times the font size without loss of content or functionality
Ambiguous links should have additional descriptions for screen readers.
Anchor links should have a descriptive label
Links should indicate their functionality and destination to assistive technology
Visual indication that a link's destination is the current page should be announced by screen readers
Warning a user that a link navigates to the homepage is recommended
Warning a user when a link triggers an image to open is recommended
Warning a user when a link triggers a mail application is recommended
Links should be tagged properly
Link context should be exposed to assistive technology
Navigation links should have a descriptive label
Warning a user when a link triggers a new browser window is recommended
Warning a user when a link triggers a PDF file is recommended
Warning a user when a link triggers a phone application is recommended
Orphaned list items with no direct list parent should not be tagged as list items
Lists should contain at least one list item
Main navigation should be labelled properly
Main navigation should have role navigation
Each area element within an image map should have a text alternative
<marquee> elements are deprecated
Avoid using role="menu" for web navigation links
Avoid using role="menubar" for web navigation links
Avoid using role="menuitem" for web navigation links
ARIA relationship and state attributes should only be applied to elements with appropriate roles
Triggers that expand additional content should expose their state to assistive technology
Menu trigger buttons/links should be tagged properly
Elements that do not expand additional content should not receive trigger attributes
aria-label should be used with compatible roles
Navigation regions should have unique labels when there are multiple navigation landmarks
List items in a navigation region should not be interactive
A list of navigation links is not contained in a navigation landmark
Navigation landmark does not contain key site navigation links
Avoid using nested navigation structure
Avoid using autofocus
The title attribute should not be the only method used to provide information
Avoid using role="application"
A slider should be operated with a single pointer
Page has a meta description
Meta viewport should allow content scaling
Pages should not use <meta http-equiv="refresh"> for automatic redirection or reloading
Page should have a title
Page title should be descriptive
Pixels should be hidden from assistive technology
Keyboard focus should move into and lock inside active dialogs
Checked state of custom radio controls should be indicated to assistive technology
Radio controls should have a label
Custom radio controls should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as radio controls should receive role="radio"
Assigned label should not be redundant
Footer region should be correctly marked up
Global site information that appears at the end of each page is contained in a contentinfo landmark (footer)
An element without global site information is tagged as a contentinfo landmark
Each page should include at most one global contentinfo landmark (footer)
Each web page that uses landmark regions should have a main landmark
All of the main content on the page is contained in the main landmark
An element without main content is tagged as a main landmark
Each page should include at most one main landmark
Main navigation region should be correctly marked up
Mandatory form fields should indicate that they are required
Original and discounted prices should be indicated to assistive technology
A search form should be tagged as a search landmark
Search results should be announced when ready
Custom select options should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as select options should be tagged as option
Skip links are the preferred technique for bypassing repeated content
Skip links should be the first elements on a page
Sliders requiring dragging movements must offer alternative single-pointer operation methods
Hidden elements should not be keyboard navigable
Strong should be tagged properly for assistive technology
SVG discernible
Tablists should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as tablists should receive role="tablist"
Tab controls should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as tabs should receive role="tab"
Tab panels should be tagged for assistive technology
Only elements that function as tab panels should receive role="tabpanel"
Non-interactive elements should not be keyboard navigable
Use the tabindex attribute correctly to manage focus for visible and hidden interactive elements
The tabindex attribute should be assigned a valid value
Table column headers should be tagged for assistive technology
Tables should include table header tags
Only elements that function as data tables should be tagged as table
Tables should not be nested
Table row headers should be tagged for assistive technology
Line height should scale to at least 1.5 times the font size without loss of content or functionality
Word spacing should scale to at least 0.16 times the font size without loss of content or functionality
The state of toggle buttons should be accurate
Toggle buttons should expose their state to assistive technology
Only elements that function as toggle buttons should be assigned the aria-pressed attribute
User ratings should be tagged and labeled for assistive technology
Visible content should not be hidden from assistive technology
Visibly hidden content should not be exposed to assistive technology
